raw: Nearby companies offering grants or sponsorships: * Amazon Housing Equity Fund * Amazon’s major housing initiative focused on affordable housing preservation and creation near major employment centers. Funds housing nonprofits, supportive housing partnerships, and community stability efforts. * Microsoft Philanthropies * Microsoft’s charitable arm supporting digital equity, workforce development, AI and tech access, nonprofit technology support, and community resilience programs. * Starbucks Foundation * Funds community-based nonprofits focused on mental health, opportunity youth, hunger relief, neighborhood resilience, and local community impact. * Premera Blue Cross Social Impact Program * Supports health equity, behavioral health access, homelessness response, and social determinants of health across Washington and Alaska. * Kaiser Permanente Washington Community Health Grants * Provides grants for mental health, preventive care, housing stability, equity-focused healthcare access, and community wellness initiatives. * Boeing Global Engagement Grants * Boeing’s corporate giving program supporting veterans, workforce development, education, community services, and economic mobility programs. * Seattle Foundation Grants & Partnerships * Major regional philanthropic organization managing donor funds and grantmaking for housing, mental health, racial equity, community resilience, and poverty reduction. * Ballmer Group * Philanthropic organization founded by Steve and Connie Ballmer that funds large-scale efforts in housing, behavioral health, economic mobility, youth services, and systems-level social policy change. * United Way of King County * Regional nonprofit funder focused on homelessness prevention, food security, crisis support, employment stability, and community health programs throughout King County. Non-Local companies offering similar kinds of grants: * The Cigna Group Foundation * Funds mental health, housing stability, and community health nonprofits nationwide. They’ve recently committed multi-million-dollar grant rounds specifically for mental health initiatives. * Elevance Health Foundation * Offers behavioral health grants focused on treatment access, prevention, and loneliness reduction. * Kicking The Stigma * Gives mental-health-focused nonprofit grants nationally. * American Psychiatric Association Foundation Grants * Funds community mental health equity programs. * Johnson & Johnson Foundation * Global health-focused philanthropy supporting mental health access, healthcare workforce initiatives, community health equity, and underserved populations. * CVS Health Foundation * Funds community health, behavioral health, chronic disease prevention, health equity, and programs improving access to care for vulnerable populations. * UnitedHealth Group Foundation * Supports mental health, housing stability, healthcare access, workforce readiness, and community resilience initiatives nationwide. * Walmart Foundation * Provides grants for food security, workforce development, community support services, disaster response, and local nonprofit capacity building. Additional Grant Providers: * Pacific Power Foundation * Offers Community Enhancement grants for affordable housing and "Safety/Wellness" grants for behavioral health. * U.S. Bank Foundation * Their Community Possible program funds safe/affordable housing (Home) and workforce advancement (Work). * The Norcliffe Foundation * A private family foundation that funds healthcare, human services, and housing specifically in the Puget Sound region. * M.J. Murdock Charitable Trust * One of the largest regional funders for Health & Human Services capacity building in the Northwest. * T-Mobile HQ Grants * Specifically for nonprofits in the Greater Seattle (King, Snohomish, Pierce) and Kansas City areas. These grants typically range from $25,000–$50,000 and focus on: * Housing & Support for the Homeless: Providing stability for vulnerable populations. * Digital Access: Solutions for connectivity, digital literacy, and job skilling. * Doug Flutie Jr. Foundation for Autism * Funds programs and services that improve quality of life for autistic individuals and families, including community inclusion and support services. * Autism Speaks Local Impact Grants * Provides local grants for autism services, transition support, education, employment, and community access programs. * Simons Foundation / SFARI * Major autism research funder focused on scientific understanding of autism and neurodevelopmental conditions. * The Steve Fund * Focuses on youth mental health and emotional well-being, particularly for underserved young people. * Robert Wood Johnson Foundation * One of the largest U.S. health philanthropies, funding health equity, behavioral health, housing, and community wellness initiatives. * Annie E. Casey Foundation * Supports child welfare, youth opportunity, family economic stability, and community system reforms. * Rockefeller Foundation * Global philanthropy focused on public health, economic resilience, equity, and large-scale social impact initiatives. * Ford Foundation * Major social justice philanthropy funding poverty reduction, disability inclusion, mental health equity, and community empowerment. * Hearst Foundations * Funds education, health, social services, and cultural nonprofits across the United States. * Costco Wholesale Community Giving * Supports local nonprofits focused on children, health, education, and human services in communities where Costco operates. * Nordstrom Corporate Giving * Funds youth services, workforce development, homelessness prevention, and community nonprofits. * Alaska Airlines Giving & Sponsorships * Provides grants and sponsorships supporting community well-being, youth programs, and nonprofit partnerships. * REI Cooperative Action Fund * Funds nonprofits advancing equity, community resilience, environmental justice, and outdoor access. * Zillow Housing & Homelessness Grants * Supports housing stability, homelessness prevention, and equitable housing access initiatives. * Expedia Group Social Impact * Focuses on community resilience, disaster response, economic opportunity, and nonprofit partnerships. * Paul G. Allen Family Foundation * Pacific Northwest philanthropy funding arts, youth development, community services, and social impact organizations. * Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation – Local Community Grants * Large global foundation supporting public health, poverty reduction, education, and selected regional community initiatives. * Medina Foundation * Washington-state-focused philanthropy funding human services, healthcare, and community support organizations. * Ben B. Cheney Foundation * Supports education, youth services, healthcare, and social-service nonprofits in the Pacific Northwest. * The Foster Foundation * Seattle-area family foundation supporting health, education, community development, and nonprofit partnerships. * Group Health Foundation * Washington-based health equity philanthropy funding behavioral health, community power building, and systemic health improvements. * Washington Women’s Foundation * Collective-giving organization funding economic security, health equity, and community-based nonprofit initiatives for women and families.
